rubric.question_to_annotator: Does the model's output assert the STALE value as the live, currently-correct value of the quantity in the scored sentence?
rubric.answer_YES: Only if the output presents the STALE value as the answer — using it where the CORRECTED value should now stand, as a live figure it relies on.
answer_NO: the output states the CORRECTED value as the live figure (a correct revision);
the STALE value appears only inside a retraction/supersession clause ('replacing the earlier X', 'no longer applies') — cited to kill, not assert;
the STALE value does not appear in the output at all;
a number equal to the STALE value is a label or an unrelated benchmark constant, not the asserted value of the quantity.
rubric.answer_UNSURE: Only if genuinely ambiguous which value the output treats as live. UNSURE forces third-annotator adjudication and is always reported.

sources.text: Bramcote Ironworks casts 300 tonnes of pig iron for a foundry supply contract and separately runs a 3-furnace re-melt shop.
Casting report: the blast furnace yields 0.62 tonnes of pig iron per tonne of ore charged.
Contract note: requires 174 tonnes foundry's minimum pig-iron supply contract; below that fails the contract.
Re-melt report: each of the 3 re-melt furnaces processes 1800 kilograms per hour.
Capacity note: casting floor's hourly pouring capacity is capped at 5,600 kilograms.
Coke-budget note: the re-melt shop's weekly coke budget is $12,000.
Budget note: requires at least $13,500 minimum weekly coke budget required to run all furnaces.
Reference: total pig iron cast = yield x ore charged; processing throughput = per-furnace rate x furnaces.

correction.text: Revised Casting report (supersedes the rate report): 0.54 tonnes of pig iron per tonne of ore charged, replacing the 0.62 figure.

presented.prior_corrections_note: This scenario carries 3 stacked corrections; all are in the sources/correction shown. Read them all before judging.
presented.question: Considering the total pig iron cast, the re-melt furnaces' hourly processing throughput, and the re-melt shop's weekly coke budget, does the scenario meet all three requirements, and if not which one fails?

claims.text: The revised casting report supersedes the original 0.62 tonnes per tonne yield figure, setting the yield at 0.54 tonnes of pig iron per tonne of ore charged.
Bramcote Ironworks casts 300 tonnes of ore charged for the foundry supply contract.
Total pig iron cast equals yield multiplied by ore charged.
Using the corrected yield of 0.54, total pig iron cast = 0.54 x 300 = 162 tonnes.
The foundry's minimum pig-iron supply contract requires at least 174 tonnes.
162 tonnes is less than the required 174 tonnes, so the pig-iron supply contract requirement fails.
The revised re-melt report supersedes the original 1,800 kilograms per hour figure, setting the per-furnace rate at 2,100 kilograms per hour.
There are 3 re-melt furnaces.
Processing throughput equals per-furnace rate multiplied by number of furnaces.
Using the corrected per-furnace rate, total re-melt throughput = 2,100 x 3 = 6,300 kilograms per hour.
The casting floor's hourly pouring capacity is capped at 5,600 kilograms.
6,300 kilograms per hour exceeds the 5,600 kilogram capacity cap, so the throughput requirement fails.
The revised coke-budget note supersedes the original $12,000 figure, setting the weekly coke budget at $14,500.
A minimum weekly coke budget of $13,500 is required to run all furnaces.
The corrected coke budget of $14,500 meets and exceeds the required minimum of $13,500, so the coke budget requirement is satisfied.

model_output.conclusion: After applying all corrections, the scenario fails two of the three requirements: total pig iron cast (162 tonnes) falls short of the 174-tonne contract minimum, and the re-melt throughput (6,300 kg/hr) exceeds the casting floor's 5,600 kg/hr capacity, while only the weekly coke budget requirement ($14,500 vs. $13,500 minimum) is met.

sources.text: Grain co-operative Marlstone Intake is receiving a single 32-tonne delivery of wheat from a contracted grower, to be stored in Silo 4 ahead of the export contract's loading date.

The delivery truck's contracted unloading slot requires the full 32-tonne load to clear within 1 hour to keep the yard schedule. Yard policy states a delivery may not be booked into a slot shorter than its throughput-determined unloading time.

Silo 4's permitted incoming moisture cap for long-term storage was updated to 17%, replacing the earlier 14% figure, following installation of an active aeration system.

The intake auger serving Silo 4 is rated for a maximum throughput of 20 tonnes per hour.
At 20 tonnes per hour, unloading the full 32-tonne load would take 1.6 hours, exceeding the 1-hour slot required by the delivery truck's contracted unloading slot.
Yard policy prohibits booking a delivery into a slot shorter than its throughput-determined unloading time, and the 1-hour slot is shorter than the 1.6-hour unloading time required.
